question: Read: 'Sensors, which can monitor your heart rate, could be embedded in your body and alert the doctor if there are any problems.' The word 'sensors' refers to devices that ________.
choices: detect and respond to physical inputs like temperature or movement
connect devices to the Internet wirelessly

question: Read: 'A smart fridge that keeps grocery lists is one example of a household appliance.' A 'household appliance' is ________.
choices: an electrical device used in the home
a handheld gadget for personal use

question: Read: 'Once a profile of your lifestyle has been built up…' This uses which passive tense?
Read: 'It is going to be shown at the New Inventors show next month.' This uses which passive form?
Read: 'Anyone who touches it is given an electric shock.' In this passive sentence, the focus is on ________.
Read: 'Within a decade or so almost every appliance in your house could be connected.' 'Could be connected' uses which passive form?
In the speaking box, the phrase 'During the period …, it is noticeable that …' is used to ________.
In the article 'Early Adopter or Laggard?', what does the article say early adopters are essentially paying for?
In the vocabulary crossword, the clue 'Not in use anymore because something newer has been invented' matches the word ________.
In the vocabulary crossword, the clue 'The ability for one piece of equipment or software to be used with another' matches the word ________.
Which word correctly completes: 'All my games are so ________ now that it probably doesn't matter.'? (The speaker means they are no longer modern.)

Read from the VR article: 'The lights are too bright. A man is shaking a pot full of coins — I think he's collecting money for charity. It's really loud. I carry on through the shopping centre. I can smell fried food.' The person in this passage is reacting to ________.
choices: excitement in a busy shopping centre
sensory overload causing anxiety in someone with ASD
boredom in an ordinary shopping trip
fear of being robbed in a public place

question: Read from the VR article: 'Those people who had been chopping down trees reached for fewer paper napkins, suggesting that their experience had subconsciously encouraged them to stop wasting paper.' The VR tree-chopping experience affected people's behaviour by making them ________.
choices: plant more trees in real life

question: In the phrasal verbs exercise, 'Experiencing something difficult can help you to grow as a person' should be replaced by ________.
In the phrasal verbs exercise, 'He stopped what he was doing to talk to his friend' should be replaced by ________.
In the phrasal verbs exercise, 'Every time she thought about it, the anxiety made her feel very bad' should be replaced by ________.

Read from the Bell article: 'In March 1876, the Scottish scientist Alexander Graham Bell called his assistant Thomas Watson and said, Mr Watson, come here, I want to see you.' What nationality was Alexander Graham Bell?

Read: 'People started taking selfies not just to present a flattering picture of themselves, but also to manage and curate what they wanted to tell the world.' In this sentence, 'flattering' means ________.

Read: 'Appearances can be deceptive. You can add filters to enhance how you look, remove any flaws or even change the shape or size of your facial features.' The word 'features' here refers to ________.
choices: special abilities of a smartphone app
parts of the face such as nose, eyes or mouth
the overall composition of a photograph

question: The word 'influential' means ________.
choices: able to change what people do or think
wanting something that someone else has

question: The word 'envious' means ________.
choices: in large quantities or for a long time
wanting something that someone else has

question: Rewrite using Pattern (a): 'Experts have estimated that the majority of people own a smartphone.' → It ________ that the majority of people own a smartphone.
In the impersonal passive grammar, Pattern (a) uses: it + passive (be + past participle) + ________.
Read from the Bell article: 'Alexander Graham Bell is most famous for inventing the telephone. He also invented a device that could be used to detect hearing problems.' What else did Bell invent?
choices: A device to detect hearing problems

question: Read: 'Although he is most famous for inventing the telephone, he also invented a device that could be used to detect hearing problems.' What does 'although' show here?
In the essay writing section, 'In my considered opinion …' is used in the ________.
Read from 'Our Personal Data': 'Most people really have very little idea how much data is being given away every time they click.' This supports which argument?
choices: Data tracking is beneficial for consumers
Data tracking is a violation of privacy because people are unaware
Data tracking is safe because it is well-regulated
Data tracking is voluntary and transparent

question: In the for-and-against essay, 'even though' is classified as a ________.
Read: 'This is clearly far more convenient, even though it is, of course, less secure.' The phrase 'even though' introduces ________.
choices: a concession or contrast to the main idea

question: According to the Life Skills section on social media, the worst kinds of comments make people feel uncomfortable or even ________.
According to the Life Skills section, what is cyberbullying?
choices: Sharing other people's private photos online
Making comments that make people feel uncomfortable or threatened
Creating fake accounts to deceive others

question: In the passive letter exercise, choose the correct form: 'Firstly, it ________ in large amounts of unnecessary plastic packaging.' (wrap)
In the passive letter exercise, choose the correct form: 'Don't you think your products ________ in a more eco-friendly way?' (should/package)
In the passive exercises, complete: 'An insect caused the malfunction.' → 'The malfunction ________ by an insect.'
In the passive exercises, 'Most people are going to use wearables in the near future.' → 'Wearables ________ by most people in the near future.'

Read: 'In conclusion, it seems obvious that while people remain largely unaware and unable to make informed choices, the ability of companies to collect data should be limited.' What writing technique is used in this conclusion?
choices: Summarising the topic and giving a clear recommendation
Repeating the introduction word for word

question: In the Life Skills section, Tip 1 'Don't post anything online without thinking about its consequences first' is matched with extra information: ________.
choices: Remember that posting privately doesn't necessarily protect you
Add new posts to move negative content further down
Set up an alert to let you know when someone mentions you
Don't share anything that belongs to anyone else without permission

question: Read: 'It is important to use social media carefully or it can have a negative effect on your life. For example, if you follow someone, make sure they are influencing you in a positive way.' This advice is from ________.
choices: the Life Skills section on social media

question: According to the Life Skills section, having a positive digital footprint is much ________ than having no digital footprint at all.
Read: 'You should also check your privacy settings as terms and conditions on websites can change, and what was private six months ago might now be more public.' This advice warns that ________.
choices: all websites must ask for your permission to change settings
privacy settings on websites can change over time without you noticing
all websites store your data permanently
social media companies always inform you of privacy changes
